content: Fungal diagnostics: 1) Blood cultures for Candida (low sensitivity ~50%, take 2-4 days); 2) Non-culture biomarkers: beta-D-glucan (elevated in most invasive fungal infections except Cryptococcus and Mucorales), galactomannan (Aspergillus-specific, serum/BAL); 3) PCR assays emerging but not standardized; 4) Imaging: CT chest for aspergillosis (halo sign early, air crescent later); 5) Histopathology from tissue biopsy gold standard (hyphae with acute angle branching for Aspergillus, pseudohyphae/yeast for Candida). T2 Candida panel detects directly from blood in 3-5 hours. Cryptococcal antigen highly sensitive/specific for cryptococcal meningitis. PJP diagnosis: induced sputum or BAL with DFA staining or PCR. Antifungal susceptibility testing guides therapy for resistant isolates (C. auris often multidrug-resistant).
